hero.title: A place for works, notes, images, travel, and process.
hero.description: This is not a feed and not a gallery. It is an evolving archive built to hold photographs, films, design, writing, travel, daily fragments, and working notes in a slower editorial rhythm.

featuredSection.title: A lead story, not a thumbnail.
featuredSection.description: The first entry point should feel like the opening spread of a magazine: one selected narrative, enough space to read, and one clear path inward.

aboutPreview: Euijoo is a developer and creative practitioner based in Seoul, building digital projects across web, media, interface, image, and writing.
The site is designed as an editorial archive rather than a portfolio wall, so the work can be arranged by story, medium, sequence, and time.

contactPreview.heading: For commissions, collaborations, and conversations.
contactPreview.description: Keep the contact area short and calm. One or two reliable paths are enough when the rest of the site already explains the tone of the work.

aboutPage.title: Work, memory, and process in one editorial system.
aboutPage.description: This site is not built as a portfolio wall. It is an archive for projects, photographs, moving image, writing, travel, daily fragments, and the notes that connect them.

sections.title: A practice shaped by making and arranging.
paragraphs: I work across web development, interface design, image-making, media editing, and writing. Rather than separating these into isolated categories, I think of them as connected forms of editorial work.
Some projects become websites. Some become moving images, notes, or image sequences. What matters most is how they are framed, read, remembered, and revisited over time.

sections.title: Why archive instead of portfolio.
paragraphs: A traditional portfolio often privileges finished outcomes. An archive makes room for process, fragments, unfinished observations, and work that gains meaning through adjacency.
Here, a travel note can sit beside a design study, and a journal entry can resonate with a later project. The structure is intended to preserve those connections instead of flattening them.

capabilities.title: Projects
capabilities.description: Case-study pages for design, web, interface, and moving-image work.

capabilities.title: Journal
capabilities.description: Essays, notes, travel logs, process records, and short reflections.

capabilities.title: Archive
capabilities.description: Images, tags, dates, media, and sequences that stay searchable over time.

principles: Consistent systems with room for variation.

contactPage.title: For commissions, collaborations, and conversations.
contactPage.description: The best contact page is clear, calm, and easy to use. A few reliable paths are enough.
notes: Available for selected digital, editorial, and archive-oriented projects.
Please include a short outline, timeline, and relevant context when reaching out.
Based in Seoul, open to remote collaboration.
